types.name: AbstractMatrix
types.javadoc_summary: A comprehensive abstract base class providing the foundational architecture for all matrix implementations in the Abacus library.

methods.name: componentType
methods.signature: @SuppressWarnings("rawtypes") public abstract Class componentType()
methods.javadoc_summary: Returns the component type of the elements in this matrix.

methods.name: array
methods.signature: @SuppressFBWarnings("EI_EXPOSE_REP") public A[] array()
methods.javadoc_summary: Returns the underlying two-dimensional array of this matrix.
contract: This method exposes the internal array representation for performance reasons and should be used with caution as modifications to the returned array will directly affect the matrix.
If you need an independent copy, use {@link #copy()} instead.

methods.name: rowCount
methods.signature: public int rowCount()
methods.javadoc_summary: Returns the number of rows in this matrix.

methods.name: columnCount
methods.signature: public int columnCount()
methods.javadoc_summary: Returns the number of columns in this matrix.

methods.name: elementCount
methods.signature: public long elementCount()
methods.javadoc_summary: Returns the total number of elements in this matrix (rows × columns).

methods.name: isEmpty
methods.signature: public boolean isEmpty()
methods.javadoc_summary: Checks if the matrix is empty (contains no elements).
contract: Checks if the matrix is empty (contains no elements).
A matrix is considered empty if either the number of rows or columns is zero, resulting in a total count of zero elements.

methods.name: println
methods.signature: public abstract String println()
methods.javadoc_summary: Prints the matrix to standard output in a formatted, human-readable manner and returns the output string.

methods.name: copy
methods.signature: public abstract X copy()
methods.javadoc_summary: Creates a copy of this matrix.

methods.name: copy
methods.signature: public abstract X copy(int fromRowIndex, int toRowIndex)

params.name: fromRowIndex
params.javadoc: the starting row index (inclusive, 0-based)

params.name: toRowIndex
params.javadoc: the ending row index (exclusive)

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates a copy of a row range from this matrix.

methods.name: copy
methods.signature: public abstract X copy(int fromRowIndex, int toRowIndex, int fromColumnIndex, int toColumnIndex)

params.name: fromRowIndex
params.javadoc: the starting row index (inclusive, 0-based)

params.name: toRowIndex
params.javadoc: the ending row index (exclusive)

params.name: fromColumnIndex
params.javadoc: the starting column index (inclusive, 0-based)

params.name: toColumnIndex
params.javadoc: the ending column index (exclusive)

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates a copy of a rectangular region from this matrix.

methods.name: rotate90
methods.signature: public abstract X rotate90()
methods.javadoc_summary: Rotates this matrix 90 degrees clockwise.

methods.name: rotate180
methods.signature: public abstract X rotate180()
methods.javadoc_summary: Rotates this matrix 180 degrees.

methods.name: rotate270
methods.signature: public abstract X rotate270()
methods.javadoc_summary: Rotates this matrix 270 degrees clockwise (or 90 degrees counter-clockwise).

methods.name: transpose
methods.signature: public abstract X transpose()
methods.javadoc_summary: Creates the transpose of this matrix by swapping rows and columns.

methods.name: reshape
methods.signature: public X reshape(final int newColumnCount)

params.name: newColumnCount
params.javadoc: the number of columns in the reshaped matrix (must be positive)

methods.javadoc_summary: Reshapes this matrix to have the specified number of columns.
contract: If the total element count is not evenly divisible by the new column count, the last row will be padded with default values (0 for numeric types, false for boolean, null for objects).

methods.name: reshape
methods.signature: public abstract X reshape(int newRowCount, int newColumnCount)

params.name: newRowCount
params.javadoc: the number of rows in the reshaped matrix (must be positive)

params.name: newColumnCount
params.javadoc: the number of columns in the reshaped matrix (must be positive)

methods.javadoc_summary: Reshapes this matrix to have the specified dimensions.
contract: If the new shape has fewer elements than the original, excess elements are discarded.
If the new shape has more elements, the extra positions are filled with default values (0 for numeric types, false for boolean, null for objects).

methods.name: isSameShape
methods.signature: public boolean isSameShape(final X x)

params.name: x
params.javadoc: the matrix to compare with

methods.javadoc_summary: Checks if this matrix has the same shape (dimensions) as another matrix.
contract: Checks if this matrix has the same shape (dimensions) as another matrix.
Two matrices have the same shape if they have the same number of rows and columns.

methods.name: repelem
methods.signature: public abstract X repelem(int rowRepeats, int colRepeats)

params.name: rowRepeats
params.javadoc: number of times to repeat each element in the row direction (must be > = 1)

params.name: colRepeats
params.javadoc: number of times to repeat each element in the column direction (must be > = 1)

methods.javadoc_summary: Repeats each element in the matrix the specified number of times in both dimensions.

methods.name: repmat
methods.signature: public abstract X repmat(int rowRepeats, int colRepeats)

params.name: rowRepeats
params.javadoc: number of times to repeat the matrix in the row direction (must be > = 1)

params.name: colRepeats
params.javadoc: number of times to repeat the matrix in the column direction (must be > = 1)

methods.javadoc_summary: Repeats the entire matrix the specified number of times in both dimensions.

methods.name: flatten
methods.signature: public abstract PL flatten()
methods.javadoc_summary: Flattens the matrix into a one-dimensional list.

methods.name: flatOp
methods.signature: public abstract <E extends Exception> void flatOp(Throwables.Consumer<? super A, E> op) throws E

params.name: op
params.javadoc: the operation to apply to the flattened array (receives array type A, not A[])

throws.condition: if the operation throws an exception

methods.javadoc_summary: Applies an operation to the flattened view of the matrix and updates the matrix with the result.

methods.name: forEach
methods.signature: public <E extends Exception> void forEach(final Throwables.IntBiConsumer<E> action) throws E

params.name: action
params.javadoc: the action to perform for each position, receives (rowIndex, columnIndex)

throws.condition: if the action throws an exception

methods.javadoc_summary: Performs the given action for each position in the matrix.
contract: This method is useful when you need to access matrix positions without caring about the actual element values, or when the element access logic is handled inside the action.

methods.name: forEach
methods.signature: public <E extends Exception> void forEach(final int fromRowIndex, final int toRowIndex, final int fromColumnIndex, final int toColumnIndex, final Throwables.IntBiConsumer<E> action) throws IndexOutOfBoundsException, E

params.name: fromRowIndex
params.javadoc: the starting row index (inclusive, 0-based)

params.name: toRowIndex
params.javadoc: the ending row index (exclusive)

params.name: fromColumnIndex
params.javadoc: the starting column index (inclusive, 0-based)

params.name: toColumnIndex
params.javadoc: the ending column index (exclusive)

params.name: action
params.javadoc: the action to perform for each position, receives (rowIndex, columnIndex)

throws.condition: if any index is out of bounds
if the action throws an exception

methods.javadoc_summary: Performs the given action for each position in the specified rectangular region of the matrix.

methods.name: forEach
methods.signature: public <E extends Exception> void forEach(final Throwables.BiIntObjConsumer<X, E> action) throws E

params.name: action
params.javadoc: the action to perform, receiving (rowIndex, columnIndex, matrix)

throws.condition: if the action throws an exception

methods.javadoc_summary: Performs the given action for each position in the matrix, providing the matrix itself as a parameter.
contract: This variant is useful when the action needs access to matrix elements or methods, allowing you to read/write values or use matrix operations within the action.

methods.name: forEach
methods.signature: public <E extends Exception> void forEach(final int fromRowIndex, final int toRowIndex, final int fromColumnIndex, final int toColumnIndex, final Throwables.BiIntObjConsumer<X, E> action) throws IndexOutOfBoundsException, E

params.name: fromRowIndex
params.javadoc: the starting row index (inclusive, 0-based)

params.name: toRowIndex
params.javadoc: the ending row index (exclusive)

params.name: fromColumnIndex
params.javadoc: the starting column index (inclusive, 0-based)

params.name: toColumnIndex
params.javadoc: the ending column index (exclusive)

params.name: action
params.javadoc: the action to perform, receiving (rowIndex, columnIndex, matrix)

throws.condition: if any index is out of bounds
if the action throws an exception

methods.javadoc_summary: Performs the given action for each position in the specified rectangular region, providing the matrix itself.

methods.name: adjacent4Points
methods.signature: public Stream<Point> adjacent4Points(final int rowIndex, final int columnIndex)

params.name: rowIndex
params.javadoc: the row index (0-based)

params.name: columnIndex
params.javadoc: the column index (0-based)

methods.javadoc_summary: Returns a stream of points adjacent to the specified position in the four cardinal directions (up, down, left, right).

methods.name: adjacent8Points
methods.signature: public Stream<Point> adjacent8Points(final int rowIndex, final int columnIndex)

params.name: rowIndex
params.javadoc: the row index (0-based)

params.name: columnIndex
params.javadoc: the column index (0-based)

methods.javadoc_summary: Returns a stream of all 8 points adjacent to the specified position, including both cardinal directions (up, down, left, right) and diagonal directions.

methods.name: pointsLU2RD
methods.signature: public Stream<Point> pointsLU2RD()
methods.javadoc_summary: Returns a stream of points along the main diagonal (left-up to right-down).
contract: The matrix must be square (rowCount == columnCount) for this operation.

methods.name: pointsRU2LD
methods.signature: public Stream<Point> pointsRU2LD()
methods.javadoc_summary: Returns a stream of points along the anti-diagonal (right-up to left-down).
contract: The matrix must be square (rowCount == columnCount) for this operation.

methods.name: pointsH
methods.signature: public Stream<Point> pointsH()
methods.javadoc_summary: Returns a stream of all points in the matrix in row-major order (horizontal traversal).

methods.name: pointsH
methods.signature: public Stream<Point> pointsH(final int rowIndex)

params.name: rowIndex
params.javadoc: the row index (0-based)

methods.javadoc_summary: Returns a stream of points for a specific row in horizontal order (left to right).

methods.name: pointsH
methods.signature: @SuppressWarnings("resource") public Stream<Point> pointsH(final int fromRowIndex, final int toRowIndex) throws IndexOutOfBoundsException

params.name: fromRowIndex
params.javadoc: the starting row index (inclusive, 0-based)

params.name: toRowIndex
params.javadoc: the ending row index (exclusive)

throws.condition: if fromRowIndex < 0, toRowIndex > rowCount, or fromRowIndex > toRowIndex

methods.javadoc_summary: Returns a stream of points for a range of rows in row-major order (horizontal traversal).

methods.name: pointsV
methods.signature: public Stream<Point> pointsV()
methods.javadoc_summary: Returns a stream of all points in the matrix in column-major order (vertical traversal).

methods.name: pointsV
methods.signature: public Stream<Point> pointsV(final int columnIndex)

params.name: columnIndex
params.javadoc: the column index (0-based)

methods.javadoc_summary: Returns a stream of points for a specific column in vertical order (top to bottom).

methods.name: pointsV
methods.signature: @SuppressWarnings("resource") public Stream<Point> pointsV(final int fromColumnIndex, final int toColumnIndex) throws IndexOutOfBoundsException

params.name: fromColumnIndex

params.name: toColumnIndex
params.javadoc: the ending column index (exclusive)

throws.condition: if fromColumnIndex < 0, toColumnIndex > columnCount, or fromColumnIndex > toColumnIndex

methods.javadoc_summary: Returns a stream of points for a range of columns in column-major order (vertical traversal).

methods.name: pointsR
methods.signature: public Stream<Stream<Point>> pointsR()
methods.javadoc_summary: Returns a stream of streams where each inner stream represents a row of points.

methods.name: pointsR
methods.signature: @SuppressWarnings("resource") public Stream<Stream<Point>> pointsR(final int fromRowIndex, final int toRowIndex) throws IndexOutOfBoundsException

params.name: fromRowIndex
params.javadoc: the starting row index (inclusive, 0-based)

params.name: toRowIndex
params.javadoc: the ending row index (exclusive)

throws.condition: if fromRowIndex < 0, toRowIndex > rowCount, or fromRowIndex > toRowIndex

methods.javadoc_summary: Returns a stream of streams for a range of rows, where each inner stream represents a row of points.

methods.name: pointsC
methods.signature: public Stream<Stream<Point>> pointsC()
methods.javadoc_summary: Returns a stream of streams where each inner stream represents a column of points.

methods.name: pointsC
methods.signature: @SuppressWarnings("resource") public Stream<Stream<Point>> pointsC(final int fromColumnIndex, final int toColumnIndex) throws IndexOutOfBoundsException

params.name: fromColumnIndex

params.name: toColumnIndex
params.javadoc: the ending column index (exclusive)

types.name: Arrays
types.javadoc_summary: A comprehensive, high-performance utility class providing extensive array manipulation and operation capabilities for all primitive types and objects in single and multi-dimensional configurations.

params.name: a
params.javadoc: the one-dimensional object array to print (can be {@code null} ).

methods.javadoc_summary: Combines elements from two boolean arrays using the provided zip function.
contract: The operation stops when the shorter array is exhausted, resulting in an output array with length equal to the minimum of the two input array lengths.
This method is useful when you want to perform element-wise operations on two arrays without worrying about length mismatches.

methods.javadoc_summary: Combines elements from two boolean arrays using the provided zip function, with default values for missing elements when arrays have different lengths.
contract: Combines elements from two boolean arrays using the provided zip function, with default values for missing elements when arrays have different lengths.
This method is ideal when you need to process arrays of different lengths and want to provide sensible defaults for missing values rather than truncating to the shorter length.

params.name: columnCount

methods.name: range
methods.signature: public static ByteMatrix range(final byte startInclusive, final byte endExclusive, final byte by)

params.name: startInclusive
params.javadoc: the starting value (inclusive)

params.name: endExclusive
params.javadoc: the ending value (exclusive)

params.name: by

contract: If the step would not reach endExclusive from startInclusive, an empty matrix is returned.

methods.name: rangeClosed
methods.signature: public static ByteMatrix rangeClosed(final byte startInclusive, final byte endInclusive)

params.name: startInclusive
params.javadoc: the starting value (inclusive)

params.name: endInclusive

params.name: startInclusive

params.name: endInclusive
params.javadoc: the ending value (inclusive, if reachable by stepping)

params.name: by
params.javadoc: the step size (must not be zero; can be positive or negative)

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates a 1-row ByteMatrix containing a closed range of byte values with a specified step.
contract: The end value is included only if it is reachable by stepping from start.
If the step would not reach endInclusive from startInclusive, an empty matrix is returned.

params.name: newRowCount
params.javadoc: the number of rows in the reshaped matrix (must be non-negative)

params.name: newColumnCount
params.javadoc: the number of columns in the reshaped matrix (must be non-negative)

methods.javadoc_summary: Reshapes the matrix to new dimensions while preserving element order.
contract:
The reshaping process follows these rules:

  • Elements are extracted from the original matrix in row-major order (left to right, top to bottom)
  • Elements are placed into the new matrix in row-major order
  • If the new shape has fewer total elements than the original, excess elements are truncated
  • If the new shape has more total elements, the additional positions are filled with zeros

Usage Examples: 

 {@code int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; int sum = 0; for (int v : values) { sum += v; } System.out.println(sum); }

Two tuples are considered equal if and only if:

  • They are of the exact same class (e.g., both CharTuple.CharTuple2)
  • They contain the same elements in the same order

This method adheres to the general contract of {@link Object#equals(Object)} .

types.name: DoubleMatrix
types.javadoc_summary: A matrix implementation for double primitive values, providing efficient storage and operations for two-dimensional double arrays.

params.javadoc: the two-dimensional double array to wrap, or null for an empty matrix

contract: If the input array is null, an empty matrix (0x0) is created instead.

contract: All rows must have the same length as the first row (rectangular array required).

contract: All rows must have the same length as the first row (rectangular array required).

contract: If both arrays are provided, they must have the same length.
The resulting matrix has dimensions n×n where n is the length of the non-null/non-empty array (or the maximum length if both are provided).

methods.name: extend
methods.signature: public DoubleMatrix extend(final int newRowCount, final int newColumnCount)

params.name: newRowCount

params.name: newColumnCount

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates a new matrix by extending or truncating this matrix to the specified dimensions.
contract: If the new dimensions are smaller than the current dimensions, the matrix is truncated.
If larger, the existing content is preserved in the top-left corner and new cells are filled with 0.0.

params.name: defaultValueForNewCell
params.javadoc: the double value to fill new cells with during extension

throws.condition: if {@code newRowCount} or {@code newColumnCount} is negative, or if the resulting matrix would be too large (dimensions exceeding Integer.MAX_VALUE elements)

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates a new matrix by extending or truncating this matrix to the specified dimensions.
contract: If the new dimensions are smaller than the current dimensions, the matrix is truncated from the top-left corner.
If larger, the existing content is preserved in the top-left corner and new cells are filled with the specified default value.

types.name: FloatMatrix
types.javadoc_summary: A matrix implementation for float primitive values, providing efficient storage and operations for two-dimensional float arrays.

methods.name: <init>
methods.signature: public FloatMatrix(final float[][] a)

params.name: a
params.javadoc: the two-dimensional float array to wrap as a matrix. Can be null, which creates an empty matrix.

methods.javadoc_summary: Constructs a FloatMatrix from a two-dimensional float array.
contract: If the input array is null, an empty matrix (0x0) is created.
If you need an independent copy, use {@link #copy()} after construction.

methods.name: empty
methods.signature: public static FloatMatrix empty()
methods.javadoc_summary: Creates an empty matrix with zero rows and zero columns.

methods.name: of
methods.signature: public static FloatMatrix of(final float[]... a)

params.name: a
params.javadoc: the two-dimensional float array to create the matrix from, or null/empty for an empty matrix

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates a FloatMatrix from a two-dimensional float array.

methods.name: from
methods.signature: public static FloatMatrix from(final int[]... a)

params.name: a
params.javadoc: the two-dimensional int array to convert to a float matrix, or null/empty for an empty matrix

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates a FloatMatrix from a two-dimensional int array by converting int values to float.
contract:
Requirements:

  • All rows must have the same length as the first row (rectangular array required)
  • The first row cannot be null if the array is non-empty

Usage Examples: 

 {@code int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; int sum = 0; for (int v : values) { sum += v; } System.out.println(sum); }

methods.name: random
methods.signature: public static FloatMatrix random(final int size)

params.name: size
params.javadoc: the number of columns in the new matrix

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates a new 1xsize matrix filled with random float values.

methods.signature: public static FloatMatrix random(final int rowCount, final int columnCount)

params.name: rowCount
params.javadoc: the number of rows in the new matrix

params.name: columnCount
params.javadoc: the number of columns in the new matrix

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates a new matrix of the specified dimensions filled with random float values.

methods.name: repeat
methods.signature: public static FloatMatrix repeat(final int rowCount, final int columnCount, final float element)

params.name: rowCount
params.javadoc: the number of rows in the new matrix

params.name: columnCount
params.javadoc: the number of columns in the new matrix

params.name: element
params.javadoc: the float value to fill the matrix with

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates a new matrix of the specified dimensions where every element is the provided {@code element} .

methods.name: diagonalLU2RD
methods.signature: public static FloatMatrix diagonalLU2RD(final float[] leftUp2RightDownDiagonal)

params.name: leftUp2RightDownDiagonal
params.javadoc: the array of main diagonal elements, or null/empty for an empty matrix

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates a square matrix from the specified main diagonal elements (left-upper to right-down).

methods.name: diagonalRU2LD
methods.signature: public static FloatMatrix diagonalRU2LD(final float[] rightUp2LeftDownDiagonal)

params.name: rightUp2LeftDownDiagonal
params.javadoc: the array of anti-diagonal elements, or null/empty for an empty matrix

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates a square matrix from the specified anti-diagonal elements (right-upper to left-down).

methods.name: diagonal
methods.signature: public static FloatMatrix diagonal(final float[] leftUp2RightDownDiagonal, final float[] rightUp2LeftDownDiagonal) throws IllegalArgumentException

params.name: leftUp2RightDownDiagonal
params.javadoc: the array of main diagonal elements (can be null or empty)

params.name: rightUp2LeftDownDiagonal
params.javadoc: the array of anti-diagonal elements (can be null or empty)

throws.condition: if both arrays are non-empty and have different lengths

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates a square matrix from the specified main diagonal and anti-diagonal elements.
contract: If both arrays are provided, they must have the same length.
The resulting matrix has dimensions n×n where n is the length of the non-null/non-empty array (or the maximum length if both are provided).

methods.javadoc_summary: Returns the element directly above the specified position, if it exists.
contract: Returns the element directly above the specified position, if it exists.
If the specified position is in the first row (i == 0), returns an empty Optional.

params.javadoc: the row index (0-based)

params.name: columnIndex

contract: Returns the element directly below the specified position, if it exists.

params.name: columnIndex

params.javadoc: the row index (0-based)

contract: Returns the element directly to the right of the specified position, if it exists.
If the specified position is in the last column, returns an empty Optional.

methods.name: row
methods.signature: public float[] row(final int rowIndex) throws IllegalArgumentException

throws.condition: if rowIndex < 0 or rowIndex > = rows

throws.condition: if rowIndex is out of bounds or row length does not match column count

contract: The source array must have exactly the same length as the number of columns in the matrix.

contract: The source array must have exactly the same length as the number of rows in the matrix.

contract: The matrix must be square (rows == columns), and the diagonal array must have exactly as many elements as the matrix has rows.

methods.signature: public <E extends Exception> void updateLU2RD(final Throwables.FloatUnaryOperator<E> operator) throws E

types.name: ImmutableIntArray
types.javadoc_summary: An immutable wrapper for primitive int arrays that provides a read-only view of the underlying array.

methods.name: of
methods.signature: public static ImmutableIntArray of(final int[] array)

params.name: array
params.javadoc: the int array to wrap, or {@code null} to create an empty ImmutableIntArray

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates an ImmutableIntArray that wraps the provided int array without copying.
contract: For true immutability, the caller must not modify the original array after passing it to this method.
If the source array might be modified externally, use {@link #copyOf(int[])} instead.
This method is more efficient than {@link #copyOf(int[])} when you know the array will not be modified, as it avoids the overhead of array copying.

methods.name: copyOf
methods.signature: public static ImmutableIntArray copyOf(final int[] array)

params.name: array
params.javadoc: the int array to copy, or {@code null} to create an empty ImmutableIntArray

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates an ImmutableIntArray containing a defensive copy of the provided array.
contract: This is the recommended factory method when the source array might be modified after creating the ImmutableIntArray, or when you need guaranteed immutability.

methods.name: get
methods.signature: public int get(final int index)

params.name: index
params.javadoc: the zero-based index of the element to retrieve (must be {@code >= 0 and < length} )

methods.javadoc_summary: Returns the int element at the specified index in this ImmutableIntArray.
methods.performance: This method provides constant-time O(1) access to elements by index.

methods.name: forEach
methods.signature: public <E extends Exception> void forEach(final Throwables.IntConsumer<E> action) throws IllegalArgumentException, E

params.name: action
params.javadoc: the action to be performed for each element, must not be {@code null}

throws.condition: if {@code action} is {@code null}
if the action throws an exception during execution

methods.javadoc_summary: Performs the given action for each element in this ImmutableIntArray.

methods.name: forEachIndexed
methods.signature: public <E extends Exception> void forEachIndexed(final Throwables.IntIntConsumer<E> action) throws IllegalArgumentException, E

params.name: action
params.javadoc: the action to be performed for each element, receiving the index (first parameter) and value (second parameter) as primitive ints, must not be {@code null}

throws.condition: if {@code action} is {@code null}
if the action throws an exception during execution

methods.javadoc_summary: Performs the given action for each element in this ImmutableIntArray, providing both the index and value.
contract: This method is useful when you need to know the position of each element during iteration, such as for creating index-based mappings or when the index is needed for computation.

methods.name: stream
methods.signature: public IntStream stream()
methods.javadoc_summary: Returns an IntStream containing all elements of this ImmutableIntArray.

methods.name: copy
methods.signature: public ImmutableIntArray copy(final int fromIndex, final int toIndex) throws IndexOutOfBoundsException

params.name: fromIndex
params.javadoc: the starting index (inclusive) of the range to copy (must be {@code >= 0} )

params.name: toIndex
params.javadoc: the ending index (exclusive) of the range to copy (must be {@code <= length} )

throws.condition: if {@code fromIndex < 0} , {@code toIndex > length} , or {@code fromIndex > toIndex}

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates a new ImmutableIntArray containing a copy of the elements in the specified range.

methods.name: copyToArray
methods.signature: public int[] copyToArray(final int fromIndex, final int toIndex) throws IndexOutOfBoundsException

params.name: fromIndex
params.javadoc: the starting index (inclusive) of the range to copy (must be {@code >= 0} )

params.name: toIndex
params.javadoc: the ending index (exclusive) of the range to copy (must be {@code <= length} )

throws.condition: if {@code fromIndex < 0} , {@code toIndex > length} , or {@code fromIndex > toIndex}

methods.javadoc_summary: Copies the elements in the specified range to a new mutable primitive int array.
contract: This is useful when you need to perform mutations on the copied data or when interfacing with APIs that require primitive arrays.

methods.name: hashCode
methods.signature: @Override public int hashCode()
methods.javadoc_summary: Returns a hash code value for this ImmutableIntArray.
contract: This method satisfies the general contract of {@link Object#hashCode()} : if two ImmutableIntArray instances are equal according to {@link #equals(Object)} , then calling this method on each will produce the same integer result.

methods.signature: @Override public boolean equals(final Object obj)

params.name: obj
params.javadoc: the object to compare with this ImmutableIntArray

methods.javadoc_summary: Compares this ImmutableIntArray with the specified object for equality.
contract: Returns {@code true} if and only if the specified object is also an ImmutableIntArray and both arrays contain the same elements in the same order.

methods.javadoc_summary: Returns a string representation of this ImmutableIntArray.

types.name: LongMatrix
types.javadoc_summary: A matrix implementation for long primitive values, providing efficient storage and operations for two-dimensional long arrays.

params.name: a
params.javadoc: the two-dimensional long array to wrap as a matrix. Can be null, which creates an empty matrix.

contract: If the input array is null, an empty matrix (0x0) is created.
If you need an independent copy, use {@link #copy()} after construction.

methods.signature: public static LongMatrix empty()
methods.javadoc_summary: Creates an empty matrix with zero rows and zero columns.

params.name: a
params.javadoc: the two-dimensional int array to convert to a long matrix, or null/empty for an empty matrix

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates a LongMatrix from a two-dimensional int array by converting int values to long.
contract: All rows must have the same length as the first row (rectangular array required).
The method validates array structure and throws an exception if the array is jagged (rows of different lengths).

params.name: element

methods.signature: public static LongMatrix range(final long startInclusive, final long endExclusive, final long by)

params.name: startInclusive
params.javadoc: the starting value (inclusive)

params.name: endExclusive
params.javadoc: the ending value (exclusive)

params.name: by

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates a 1-row LongMatrix with values from startInclusive to endExclusive with the specified step.
contract: If the step would not reach endExclusive from startInclusive, an empty matrix is returned.

methods.name: rangeClosed
methods.signature: public static LongMatrix rangeClosed(final long startInclusive, final long endInclusive)

params.name: startInclusive
params.javadoc: the starting value (inclusive)

params.name: endInclusive
params.javadoc: the ending value (inclusive)

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates a 1-row LongMatrix with values from startInclusive to endInclusive.
contract: If {@code startInclusive > endInclusive} , an empty matrix is returned.

methods.name: rangeClosed
methods.signature: public static LongMatrix rangeClosed(final long startInclusive, final long endInclusive, final long by)

params.name: startInclusive
params.javadoc: the starting value (inclusive)

params.name: endInclusive
params.javadoc: the ending value (inclusive, if reachable by stepping)

params.name: by
params.javadoc: the step size (must not be zero; can be positive or negative)

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates a 1-row LongMatrix with values from startInclusive to endInclusive with the specified step.
contract: The end value is included only if it is reachable by stepping from start.
If the step would not reach endInclusive from startInclusive, an empty matrix is returned.

contract: If both arrays are provided, they must have the same length.
The resulting matrix has dimensions n×n where n is the length of the non-null/non-empty array (or the maximum length if both are provided).

methods.name: median
methods.signature: public long median()
methods.javadoc_summary: Returns the median long value in this tuple.
contract:
The median is calculated as follows:

  • For tuples with an odd number of elements, returns the middle value when sorted
  • For tuples with an even number of elements, returns the lower middle value when sorted

Usage Examples: 

 {@code int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; int sum = 0; for (int v : values) { sum += v; } System.out.println(sum); }

types.name: Matrix
types.javadoc_summary: A generic matrix implementation that stores elements in a two-dimensional array.

methods.name: <init>
methods.signature: public Matrix(final T[][] a)

params.name: a
params.javadoc: the two-dimensional array of elements (must not be null)

methods.javadoc_summary: Constructs a Matrix from a two-dimensional array.
contract: The array must be rectangular (all rows must have the same length).

methods.name: of
methods.signature: @SafeVarargs public static <T> Matrix<T> of(final T[]... a)

params.name: a
params.javadoc: the two-dimensional array to create the matrix from (must not be null)

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates a Matrix from a two-dimensional array.
contract: All rows must have the same length as the first row (rectangular array required).
The array must not be null.

methods.name: repeat
methods.signature: public static <T> Matrix<T> repeat(final int rowCount, final int columnCount, final T element) throws IllegalArgumentException

params.name: rowCount
params.javadoc: the number of rows in the new matrix

params.name: columnCount
params.javadoc: the number of columns in the new matrix

params.name: element
params.javadoc: the value to fill the matrix with (must not be null)

throws.condition: if rowCount or columnCount is negative, or if element is null

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates a new matrix of the specified dimensions where every element is the provided {@code element} .

methods.name: diagonalLU2RD
methods.signature: public static <T> Matrix<T> diagonalLU2RD(final T[] leftUp2RightDownDiagonal)

params.name: leftUp2RightDownDiagonal
params.javadoc: the diagonal values (must not be null)

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates a square diagonal matrix with the given values on the main diagonal (left-up to right-down).

methods.name: diagonalRU2LD
methods.signature: public static <T> Matrix<T> diagonalRU2LD(final T[] rightUp2LeftDownDiagonal)

params.name: rightUp2LeftDownDiagonal
params.javadoc: the anti-diagonal values (must not be null)

methods.name: diagonal
methods.signature: @SuppressWarnings("null") public static <T> Matrix<T> diagonal(final T[] leftUp2RightDownDiagonal, final T[] rightUp2LeftDownDiagonal) throws IllegalArgumentException

params.name: leftUp2RightDownDiagonal
params.javadoc: the main diagonal values.

params.name: rightUp2LeftDownDiagonal
params.javadoc: the anti-diagonal values.

throws.condition: if both arrays are null, or if both diagonals are non-empty and have different lengths

methods.javadoc_summary: Creates a square matrix with values on both diagonals.
contract: If diagonals intersect (odd dimension), the main diagonal value takes precedence.
At least one diagonal must be non-null.

types.name: Matrices
types.javadoc_summary: Utility class providing static methods for matrix operations, parallel processing control, and matrix manipulation functions.

methods.name: getParallelEnabled
methods.signature: public static ParallelEnabled getParallelEnabled()
methods.javadoc_summary: Returns the current parallel processing setting for the current thread.
contract:
The returned value indicates how matrix operations should decide whether to use parallel processing:

  • {@link ParallelEnabled#YES} - Forces parallel execution regardless of matrix size
  • {@link ParallelEnabled#NO} - Forces sequential execution regardless of matrix size
  • {@link ParallelEnabled#DEFAULT} - Automatically decides based on matrix size (threshold: 8192 elements)

Usage Examples: 

 {@code int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; int sum = 0; for (int v : values) { sum += v; } System.out.println(sum); }

params.name: flag

methods.javadoc_summary: Determines whether a matrix operation should be processed using parallel execution based on the element count and current parallel settings.
contract: Determines whether a matrix operation should be processed using parallel execution based on the element count and current parallel settings.

This method makes the parallelization decision using a multifactor evaluation:

  1. Runtime Support: Parallel streams must be available in the runtime environment.
If not supported, always returns {@code false} .

• Thread Setting: Checks the current thread's {@link ParallelEnabled} setting:
    • {@link ParallelEnabled#YES} - Always returns {@code true} (if runtime supports it)
    • {@link ParallelEnabled#NO} - Always returns {@code false}
    • {@link ParallelEnabled#DEFAULT} - Decides based on element count
  • Element Count: When using {@code DEFAULT} setting, returns {@code true} only if {@code count >= 8192} .

Special cases:

  • Single matrix: Returns {@code true} (trivially same shape)
  • Multiple matrices: Returns {@code true} only if all have identical dimensions

methods.javadoc_summary: Executes a command for each position in a specified subregion of a matrix grid.
contract:
Iteration strategy:

  • If there are fewer or equal rows than columns, iterates by rows first (row-major order)
  • If there are more rows than columns, iterates by columns first (column-major order)
  • When parallel execution is enabled, the outer loop is parallelized while the inner loop remains sequential

Usage Examples: 

 {@code int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; int sum = 0; for (int v : values) { sum += v; } System.out.println(sum); }

methods.javadoc_summary: Performs matrix multiplication iteration using a custom accumulator function.
contract: It does NOT perform the actual multiplication arithmetic - that must be implemented in the command function.

For standard matrix multiplication C = A × B, the command would typically accumulate: {@code C[i][j] += A[i][k] * B[k][j]}

Index meanings:

  • {@code i} - Row index in matrix A (and result matrix C)
  • {@code j} - Column index in matrix B (and result matrix C)
  • {@code k} - Common dimension (columns in A, rows in B)

The matrices must satisfy the multiplication constraint: {@code a.columnCount == b.rowCount} .

methods.javadoc_summary: Combines multiple {@link ByteMatrix} objects element-wise using a function that operates on byte arrays, with control over intermediate array sharing.
contract:
The {@code shareIntermediateArray} parameter controls memory optimization:

  • {@code true} and sequential execution: Reuses the same intermediate array for all positions, reducing memory allocations but requiring the zip function to not retain references to the array
  • {@code false} or parallel execution: Creates a new array for each position, safer but uses more memory

Warning: When {@code shareIntermediateArray} is {@code true} , the zip function must NOT store references to the array, as it will be mutated for subsequent positions.
Only use this optimization if the function immediately processes and discards the array.

methods.javadoc_summary: Combines multiple generic {@link Matrix} objects element-wise using a function that operates on arrays, with control over intermediate array sharing.
contract:
The {@code shareIntermediateArray} parameter controls memory optimization:

  • {@code true} and sequential execution: Reuses the same intermediate array for all positions, reducing memory allocations but requiring the zip function to not retain references to the array
  • {@code false} or parallel execution: Creates a new array for each position, safer but uses more memory

Warning: When {@code shareIntermediateArray} is {@code true} , the zip function must NOT store references to the array, as it will be mutated for subsequent positions.
Only use this optimization if the function immediately processes and discards the array.

types.name: ParallelEnabled
types.javadoc_summary: Enumeration that controls parallel processing behavior for matrix operations.

fields.name: YES
fields.javadoc_summary: Forces parallel execution of matrix operations regardless of matrix size.

fields.name: NO
fields.javadoc_summary: Forces sequential execution of matrix operations.

fields.name: DEFAULT
fields.javadoc_summary: Uses default heuristics to determine whether to parallelize.

types.name: Points
types.javadoc_summary: Utility class containing point classes for two-dimensional and three-dimensional coordinate systems.

types.name: xy
types.javadoc_summary: This class provides two-dimensional coordinate point implementations.

types.name: ByteBytePoint
types.javadoc_summary: Represents an immutable two-dimensional point with byte coordinates and a byte value.

methods.name: <init>
methods.signature:
record ByteBytePoint(byte x, byte y, byte v) { /** * Creates a new ByteBytePoint with the specified coordinates and value. * This factory method provides a convenient way to construct two-dimensional points * with byte-range coordinates and values, optimized for memory efficiency. * *

Usage Examples:

* 
{@code * int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; * int sum = 0; * for (int v : values) { * sum += v; * } * System.out.println(sum); * }
* @param x the x-coordinate of the point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @param y the y-coordinate of the point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @param v the value associated with this point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @return a new ByteBytePoint instance with the specified coordinates and value, never {@code null} */ public static ByteBytePoint of(final byte x, final byte y, final byte v) { return new ByteBytePoint(x, y, v); } } /** * Represents an immutable two-dimensional point with byte coordinates and an integer value. * This class is useful when coordinates are constrained to byte range (-128 to 127) * but the associated value requires the full integer range. * *

All instances are immutable and thread-safe.

* * @param x the x-coordinate of this point * @param y the y-coordinate of this point * @param v the value associated with this point */ public record ByteIntPoint(byte x, byte y, int v) { /** * Creates a new ByteIntPoint with the specified coordinates and value. * This factory method is ideal when working with small-range coordinates but requiring * a full integer range for the associated value, such as counting or indexing operations. * *

Usage Examples:

* 
{@code * int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; * int sum = 0; * for (int v : values) { * sum += v; * } * System.out.println(sum); * }
* @param x the x-coordinate of the point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @param y the y-coordinate of the point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @param v the integer value associated with this point * @return a new ByteIntPoint instance with the specified coordinates and value, never {@code null} */ public static ByteIntPoint of(final byte x, final byte y, final int v) { return new ByteIntPoint(x, y, v); } } /** * Represents an immutable two-dimensional point with byte coordinates and a long value. * This class is useful when coordinates are constrained to byte range (-128 to 127) * but the associated value requires the full long integer range. * *

All instances are immutable and thread-safe.

* * @param x the x-coordinate of this point * @param y the y-coordinate of this point * @param v the value associated with this point */ public record ByteLongPoint(byte x, byte y, long v) { /** * Creates a new ByteLongPoint with the specified coordinates and value. * This factory method is ideal when working with small-range coordinates but requiring * a full long integer range for the associated value, such as timestamps or large counts. * *

methods.name: <init>
methods.signature:
record ByteBytePoint(byte x, byte y, byte z, byte v) { /** * Creates a new ByteBytePoint with the specified x, y, z coordinates and value. * This factory method provides a convenient way to construct three-dimensional points * with byte-range coordinates and values, optimized for maximum memory efficiency. * *

Usage Examples:

* 
{@code * int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; * int sum = 0; * for (int v : values) { * sum += v; * } * System.out.println(sum); * }
* @param x the x-coordinate of the point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @param y the y-coordinate of the point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @param z the z-coordinate of the point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @param v the value associated with this point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @return a new ByteBytePoint instance with the specified coordinates and value, never {@code null} */ public static ByteBytePoint of(final byte x, final byte y, final byte z, final byte v) { return new ByteBytePoint(x, y, z, v); } } /** * Represents an immutable three-dimensional point with byte coordinates and an integer value. * This class is useful when coordinates are constrained to byte range (-128 to 127) * but the associated value requires the full integer range. * *

All instances are immutable and thread-safe.

* * @param x the x-coordinate of this point * @param y the y-coordinate of this point * @param z the z-coordinate of this point * @param v the value associated with this point */ public record ByteIntPoint(byte x, byte y, byte z, int v) { /** * Creates a new ByteIntPoint with the specified coordinates and value. * This factory method is ideal when working with small-range 3D coordinates but requiring * a full integer range for the associated value, such as counting or indexing in 3D space. * *

Usage Examples:

* 
{@code * int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; * int sum = 0; * for (int v : values) { * sum += v; * } * System.out.println(sum); * }
* @param x the x-coordinate of the point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @param y the y-coordinate of the point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @param z the z-coordinate of the point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @param v the integer value associated with this point * @return a new ByteIntPoint instance with the specified coordinates and value, never {@code null} */ public static ByteIntPoint of(final byte x, final byte y, final byte z, final int v) { return new ByteIntPoint(x, y, z, v); } } /** * Represents an immutable three-dimensional point with byte coordinates and a long value. * This class is useful when coordinates are constrained to byte range (-128 to 127) * but the associated value requires the full long integer range. * *

All instances are immutable and thread-safe.

* * @param x the x-coordinate of this point * @param y the y-coordinate of this point * @param z the z-coordinate of this point * @param v the value associated with this point */ public record ByteLongPoint(byte x, byte y, byte z, long v) { /** * Creates a new ByteLongPoint with the specified coordinates and value. * This factory method is ideal when working with small-range 3D coordinates but requiring * a full long integer range for the associated value, such as timestamps or large identifiers in 3D space. * *

Usage Examples:

* 
{@code * int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; * int sum = 0; * for (int v : values) { * sum += v; * } * System.out.println(sum); * }
* @param x the x-coordinate of the point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @param y the y-coordinate of the point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @param z the z-coordinate of the point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @param v the long value associated with this point * @return a new ByteLongPoint instance with the specified coordinates and value, never {@code null} */ public static ByteLongPoint of(final byte x, final byte y, final byte z, final long v) { return new ByteLongPoint(x, y, z, v); } } /** * Represents an immutable three-dimensional point with byte coordinates and a double-precision floating-point value. * This class is useful when coordinates are constrained to byte range (-128 to 127) * but the associated value requires floating-point precision. * *

All instances are immutable and thread-safe.

* * @param x the x-coordinate of this point * @param y the y-coordinate of this point * @param z the z-coordinate of this point * @param v the value associated with this point */ public record ByteDoublePoint(byte x, byte y, byte z, double v) { /** * Creates a new ByteDoublePoint with the specified coordinates and value. * This factory method is ideal when working with small-range 3D coordinates but requiring * floating-point precision for the associated value, such as densities, probabilities, or physical measurements in 3D space. * *

Usage Examples:

* 
{@code * int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; * int sum = 0; * for (int v : values) { * sum += v; * } * System.out.println(sum); * }
* @param x the x-coordinate of the point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @param y the y-coordinate of the point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @param z the z-coordinate of the point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @param v the double value associated with this point * @return a new ByteDoublePoint instance with the specified coordinates and value, never {@code null} */ public static ByteDoublePoint of(final byte x, final byte y, final byte z, final double v) { return new ByteDoublePoint(x, y, z, v); } } /** * Represents an immutable three-dimensional point with byte coordinates and a generic object value. * This class is useful when coordinates are constrained to byte range (-128 to 127) * but the associated value can be any object type. * *

All instances are immutable and thread-safe.

* * @param the type of the value object associated with this point * @param x the x-coordinate of this point * @param y the y-coordinate of this point * @param z the z-coordinate of this point * @param v the value associated with this point */ public record ByteObjPoint(byte x, byte y, byte z, T v) { /** * Creates a new ByteObjPoint with the specified coordinates and value. * This factory method is ideal when working with small-range 3D coordinates but requiring * any object type for the associated value, providing maximum flexibility for storing * complex data structures at specific 3D positions. * *

Usage Examples:

* 
{@code * int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; * int sum = 0; * for (int v : values) { * sum += v; * } * System.out.println(sum); * }
* @param the type of the value associated with this point * @param x the x-coordinate of the point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @param y the y-coordinate of the point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @param z the z-coordinate of the point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @param v the object value associated with this point, may be {@code null} * @return a new ByteObjPoint instance with the specified coordinates and value, never {@code null} */ public static ByteObjPoint of(final byte x, final byte y, final byte z, final T v) { return new ByteObjPoint<>(x, y, z, v); } } /** * Represents an immutable three-dimensional point with integer coordinates and a byte value. * This class is useful when coordinates require the full integer range * but the associated value is constrained to byte range (-128 to 127). * *

All instances are immutable and thread-safe.

* * @param x the x-coordinate of this point * @param y the y-coordinate of this point * @param z the z-coordinate of this point * @param v the value associated with this point */ public record IntBytePoint(int x, int y, int z, byte v) { /** * Creates a new IntBytePoint with the specified coordinates and value. * This factory method is ideal when working with standard integer 3D coordinates but the * associated value fits within a byte range, providing memory efficiency for the value component. * *

Usage Examples:

* 
{@code * int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; * int sum = 0; * for (int v : values) { * sum += v; * } * System.out.println(sum); * }
* @param x the x-coordinate of the point * @param y the y-coordinate of the point * @param z the z-coordinate of the point * @param v the byte value associated with this point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @return a new IntBytePoint instance with the specified coordinates and value, never {@code null} */ public static IntBytePoint of(final int x, final int y, final int z, final byte v) { return new IntBytePoint(x, y, z, v); } } /** * Represents an immutable three-dimensional point with integer coordinates and an integer value. * This class is the most commonly used point type for general-purpose integer-based * coordinate systems and grid operations. * *

All instances are immutable and thread-safe.

* * @param x the x-coordinate of this point * @param y the y-coordinate of this point * @param z the z-coordinate of this point * @param v the value associated with this point */ public record IntIntPoint(int x, int y, int z, int v) { /** * Creates a new IntIntPoint with the specified coordinates and value. * This is the most commonly used 3D point type for general-purpose integer-based * coordinate systems, providing a balanced approach with full integer range for * all three coordinates and the associated value. * *

Usage Examples:

* 
{@code * int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; * int sum = 0; * for (int v : values) { * sum += v; * } * System.out.println(sum); * }
* @param x the x-coordinate of the point * @param y the y-coordinate of the point * @param z the z-coordinate of the point * @param v the integer value associated with this point * @return a new IntIntPoint instance with the specified coordinates and value, never {@code null} */ public static IntIntPoint of(final int x, final int y, final int z, final int v) { return new IntIntPoint(x, y, z, v); } } /** * Represents an immutable three-dimensional point with integer coordinates and a long value. * This class is useful when coordinates fit within the integer range * but the associated value requires the full long integer range. * *

All instances are immutable and thread-safe.

* * @param x the x-coordinate of this point * @param y the y-coordinate of this point * @param z the z-coordinate of this point * @param v the value associated with this point */ public record IntLongPoint(int x, int y, int z, long v) { /** * Creates a new IntLongPoint with the specified coordinates and value. * This factory method is ideal when working with standard integer 3D coordinates but requiring * a long value for timestamps, large counts, or identifiers that exceed the integer range. * *

Usage Examples:

* 
{@code * int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; * int sum = 0; * for (int v : values) { * sum += v; * } * System.out.println(sum); * }
* @param x the x-coordinate of the point * @param y the y-coordinate of the point * @param z the z-coordinate of the point * @param v the long value associated with this point * @return a new IntLongPoint instance with the specified coordinates and value, never {@code null} */ public static IntLongPoint of(final int x, final int y, final int z, final long v) { return new IntLongPoint(x, y, z, v); } } /** * Represents an immutable three-dimensional point with integer coordinates and a double-precision floating-point value. * This class is useful when coordinates fit within the integer range * but the associated value requires floating-point precision. * *

All instances are immutable and thread-safe.

* * @param x the x-coordinate of this point * @param y the y-coordinate of this point * @param z the z-coordinate of this point * @param v the value associated with this point */ public record IntDoublePoint(int x, int y, int z, double v) { /** * Creates a new IntDoublePoint with the specified coordinates and value. * This factory method is ideal when working with standard integer 3D grid coordinates but requiring * floating-point precision for the associated value, such as densities, distances, or probabilities. * *

Usage Examples:

* 
{@code * int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; * int sum = 0; * for (int v : values) { * sum += v; * } * System.out.println(sum); * }
* @param x the x-coordinate of the point * @param y the y-coordinate of the point * @param z the z-coordinate of the point * @param v the double value associated with this point * @return a new IntDoublePoint instance with the specified coordinates and value, never {@code null} */ public static IntDoublePoint of(final int x, final int y, final int z, final double v) { return new IntDoublePoint(x, y, z, v); } } /** * Represents an immutable three-dimensional point with integer coordinates and a generic object value. * This class is useful when coordinates fit within the integer range * but the associated value can be any object type. * *

All instances are immutable and thread-safe.

* * @param the type of the value object associated with this point * @param x the x-coordinate of this point * @param y the y-coordinate of this point * @param z the z-coordinate of this point * @param v the value associated with this point */ public record IntObjPoint(int x, int y, int z, T v) { /** * Creates a new IntObjPoint with the specified coordinates and value. * This factory method provides maximum flexibility by allowing any object type as the value * while using standard integer 3D coordinates, making it suitable for complex voxel-based data structures. * *

Usage Examples:

* 
{@code * int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; * int sum = 0; * for (int v : values) { * sum += v; * } * System.out.println(sum); * }
* @param the type of the value associated with this point * @param x the x-coordinate of the point * @param y the y-coordinate of the point * @param z the z-coordinate of the point * @param v the object value associated with this point, may be {@code null} * @return a new IntObjPoint instance with the specified coordinates and value, never {@code null} */ public static IntObjPoint of(final int x, final int y, final int z, final T v) { return new IntObjPoint<>(x, y, z, v); } } /** * Represents an immutable three-dimensional point with long coordinates and a byte value. * This class is useful when coordinates require the full long integer range * but the associated value is constrained to byte range (-128 to 127). * *

All instances are immutable and thread-safe.

* * @param x the x-coordinate of this point * @param y the y-coordinate of this point * @param z the z-coordinate of this point * @param v the value associated with this point */ public record LongBytePoint(long x, long y, long z, byte v) { /** * Creates a new LongBytePoint with the specified coordinates and value. * This factory method is ideal when working with very large 3D coordinate spaces requiring * long values but the associated value fits within a byte range, optimizing memory for the value component. * *

Usage Examples:

* 
{@code * int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; * int sum = 0; * for (int v : values) { * sum += v; * } * System.out.println(sum); * }
* @param x the x-coordinate of the point * @param y the y-coordinate of the point * @param z the z-coordinate of the point * @param v the byte value associated with this point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @return a new LongBytePoint instance with the specified coordinates and value, never {@code null} */ public static LongBytePoint of(final long x, final long y, final long z, final byte v) { return new LongBytePoint(x, y, z, v); } } /** * Represents an immutable three-dimensional point with long coordinates and an integer value. * This class is useful when coordinates require the full long integer range * but the associated value fits within the integer range. * *

All instances are immutable and thread-safe.

* * @param x the x-coordinate of this point * @param y the y-coordinate of this point * @param z the z-coordinate of this point * @param v the value associated with this point */ public record LongIntPoint(long x, long y, long z, int v) { /** * Creates a new LongIntPoint with the specified coordinates and value. * This factory method is ideal when working with very large 3D coordinate spaces requiring * long values but the associated value fits within the integer range. * *

Usage Examples:

* 
{@code * int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; * int sum = 0; * for (int v : values) { * sum += v; * } * System.out.println(sum); * }
* @param x the x-coordinate of the point * @param y the y-coordinate of the point * @param z the z-coordinate of the point * @param v the integer value associated with this point * @return a new LongIntPoint instance with the specified coordinates and value, never {@code null} */ public static LongIntPoint of(final long x, final long y, final long z, final int v) { return new LongIntPoint(x, y, z, v); } } /** * Represents an immutable three-dimensional point with long coordinates and a long value. * This class provides full long integer range for both coordinates and associated value. * *

All instances are immutable and thread-safe.

* * @param x the x-coordinate of this point * @param y the y-coordinate of this point * @param z the z-coordinate of this point * @param v the value associated with this point */ public record LongLongPoint(long x, long y, long z, long v) { /** * Creates a new LongLongPoint with the specified coordinates and value. * This factory method provides the maximum integer range for both 3D coordinates and the * associated value, suitable for very large coordinate spaces with large identifiers or timestamps. * *

Usage Examples:

* 
{@code * int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; * int sum = 0; * for (int v : values) { * sum += v; * } * System.out.println(sum); * }
* @param x the x-coordinate of the point * @param y the y-coordinate of the point * @param z the z-coordinate of the point * @param v the long value associated with this point * @return a new LongLongPoint instance with the specified coordinates and value, never {@code null} */ public static LongLongPoint of(final long x, final long y, final long z, final long v) { return new LongLongPoint(x, y, z, v); } } /** * Represents an immutable three-dimensional point with long coordinates and a double-precision floating-point value. * This class is useful when coordinates require the full long integer range * but the associated value requires floating-point precision. * *

All instances are immutable and thread-safe.

* * @param x the x-coordinate of this point * @param y the y-coordinate of this point * @param z the z-coordinate of this point * @param v the value associated with this point */ public record LongDoublePoint(long x, long y, long z, double v) { /** * Creates a new LongDoublePoint with the specified coordinates and value. * This factory method is ideal when working with very large 3D coordinate spaces requiring * long values but needing floating-point precision for the associated value. * *

Usage Examples:

* 
{@code * int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; * int sum = 0; * for (int v : values) { * sum += v; * } * System.out.println(sum); * }
* @param x the x-coordinate of the point * @param y the y-coordinate of the point * @param z the z-coordinate of the point * @param v the double value associated with this point * @return a new LongDoublePoint instance with the specified coordinates and value, never {@code null} */ public static LongDoublePoint of(final long x, final long y, final long z, final double v) { return new LongDoublePoint(x, y, z, v); } } /** * Represents an immutable three-dimensional point with long coordinates and a generic object value. * This class is useful when coordinates require the full long integer range * but the associated value can be any object type. * *

All instances are immutable and thread-safe.

* * @param the type of the value object associated with this point * @param x the x-coordinate of this point * @param y the y-coordinate of this point * @param z the z-coordinate of this point * @param v the value associated with this point */ public record LongObjPoint(long x, long y, long z, T v) { /** * Creates a new LongObjPoint with the specified coordinates and value. * This factory method provides maximum flexibility by allowing any object type as the value * while using long integer 3D coordinates, suitable for very large coordinate spaces with complex data. * *

Usage Examples:

* 
{@code * int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; * int sum = 0; * for (int v : values) { * sum += v; * } * System.out.println(sum); * }
* @param the type of the value associated with this point * @param x the x-coordinate of the point * @param y the y-coordinate of the point * @param z the z-coordinate of the point * @param v the object value associated with this point, may be {@code null} * @return a new LongObjPoint instance with the specified coordinates and value, never {@code null} */ public static LongObjPoint of(final long x, final long y, final long z, final T v) { return new LongObjPoint<>(x, y, z, v); } } /** * Represents an immutable three-dimensional point with double-precision floating-point coordinates and a byte value. * This class is useful when coordinates require floating-point precision * but the associated value is constrained to byte range (-128 to 127). * *

All instances are immutable and thread-safe.

* * @param x the x-coordinate of this point * @param y the y-coordinate of this point * @param z the z-coordinate of this point * @param v the value associated with this point */ public record DoubleBytePoint(double x, double y, double z, byte v) { /** * Creates a new DoubleBytePoint with the specified coordinates and value. * This factory method is ideal when working with floating-point 3D coordinate spaces but the * associated value fits within a byte range, optimizing memory for the value component. * *

Usage Examples:

* 
{@code * int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; * int sum = 0; * for (int v : values) { * sum += v; * } * System.out.println(sum); * }
* @param x the x-coordinate of the point * @param y the y-coordinate of the point * @param z the z-coordinate of the point * @param v the byte value associated with this point, must be in the range [-128, 127] * @return a new DoubleBytePoint instance with the specified coordinates and value, never {@code null} */ public static DoubleBytePoint of(final double x, final double y, final double z, final byte v) { return new DoubleBytePoint(x, y, z, v); } } /** * Represents an immutable three-dimensional point with double-precision floating-point coordinates and an integer value. * This class is useful when coordinates require floating-point precision * but the associated value fits within the integer range. * *

All instances are immutable and thread-safe.

* * @param x the x-coordinate of this point * @param y the y-coordinate of this point * @param z the z-coordinate of this point * @param v the value associated with this point */ public record DoubleIntPoint(double x, double y, double z, int v) { /** * Creates a new DoubleIntPoint with the specified coordinates and value. * This factory method is ideal when working with floating-point 3D coordinate spaces but the * associated value fits within the integer range, such as counts or indices in continuous 3D spaces. * *

Usage Examples:

* 
{@code * int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; * int sum = 0; * for (int v : values) { * sum += v; * } * System.out.println(sum); * }
* @param x the x-coordinate of the point * @param y the y-coordinate of the point * @param z the z-coordinate of the point * @param v the integer value associated with this point * @return a new DoubleIntPoint instance with the specified coordinates and value, never {@code null} */ public static DoubleIntPoint of(final double x, final double y, final double z, final int v) { return new DoubleIntPoint(x, y, z, v); } } /** * Represents an immutable three-dimensional point with double-precision floating-point coordinates and a long value. * This class is useful when coordinates require floating-point precision * but the associated value requires the full long integer range. * *

All instances are immutable and thread-safe.

* * @param x the x-coordinate of this point * @param y the y-coordinate of this point * @param z the z-coordinate of this point * @param v the value associated with this point */ public record DoubleLongPoint(double x, double y, double z, long v) { /** * Creates a new DoubleLongPoint with the specified coordinates and value. * This factory method is ideal when working with floating-point 3D coordinate spaces and requiring * long values for timestamps, large identifiers, or counts that exceed the integer range. * *

Usage Examples:

* 
{@code * int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; * int sum = 0; * for (int v : values) { * sum += v; * } * System.out.println(sum); * }
* @param x the x-coordinate of the point * @param y the y-coordinate of the point * @param z the z-coordinate of the point * @param v the long value associated with this point * @return a new DoubleLongPoint instance with the specified coordinates and value, never {@code null} */ public static DoubleLongPoint of(final double x, final double y, final double z, final long v) { return new DoubleLongPoint(x, y, z, v); } } /** * Represents an immutable three-dimensional point with double-precision floating-point coordinates and value. * This class provides full double-precision floating-point support for both coordinates and the associated value. * *

All instances are immutable and thread-safe.

* * @param x the x-coordinate of this point * @param y the y-coordinate of this point * @param z the z-coordinate of this point * @param v the value associated with this point */ public record DoubleDoublePoint(double x, double y, double z, double v) { /** * Creates a new DoubleDoublePoint with the specified coordinates and value. * This factory method provides full double-precision floating-point support for both 3D coordinates * and the associated value, ideal for continuous mathematical, physical, and scientific 3D applications. * *

Usage Examples:

* 
{@code * int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; * int sum = 0; * for (int v : values) { * sum += v; * } * System.out.println(sum); * }
* @param x the x-coordinate of the point * @param y the y-coordinate of the point * @param z the z-coordinate of the point * @param v the double value associated with this point * @return a new DoubleDoublePoint instance with the specified coordinates and value, never {@code null} */ public static DoubleDoublePoint of(final double x, final double y, final double z, final double v) { return new DoubleDoublePoint(x, y, z, v); } } /** * Represents an immutable three-dimensional point with double-precision floating-point coordinates and a generic object value. * This class is useful when coordinates require floating-point precision * but the associated value can be any object type. * *

All instances are immutable and thread-safe.

* * @param the type of the value object associated with this point * @param x the x-coordinate of this point * @param y the y-coordinate of this point * @param z the z-coordinate of this point * @param v the value associated with this point */ public record DoubleObjPoint(double x, double y, double z, T v) { /** * Creates a new DoubleObjPoint with the specified coordinates and value. * This factory method provides maximum flexibility by allowing any object type as the value * while using double-precision floating-point 3D coordinates, suitable for continuous spaces with complex data. * *

Usage Examples:

* 
{@code * int[] values = { 1, 2, 3 }; * int sum = 0; * for (int v : values) { * sum += v; * } * System.out.println(sum); * }
* @param the type of the value associated with this point * @param x the x-coordinate of the point * @param y the y-coordinate of the point * @param z the z-coordinate of the point * @param v the object value associated with this point, may be {@code null} * @return a new DoubleObjPoint instance with the specified coordinates and value, never {@code null} */ public static DoubleObjPoint of(final double x, final double y, final double z, final T v) { return new DoubleObjPoint<>(x, y, z, v); } } } }
